title: myristica fugax subsp. fugax
intro: Myristica fugax subsp. fugax, commonly known as the false nutmeg, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myristicaceae. The plant is known for its aromatic seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The seeds have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and are said to have health benefits including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in digestion and improving immune function. The seeds are often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are also used in traditional cooking and as a spice.
